CONTENT.Club career: In June 2021, Schalke 04, newly relegated to the 2. Bundesliga, announced the signing of Ranftl from LASK on a three-year contract. In June 2022, he agreed to join Austria Wien on loan for the 2022–23 season with an option to make the move permanent. In June 2023, he completed the permanent move.
CONTENT.External links: Reinhold Ranftl at National-Football-Teams.comReinhold Ranftl at Austrian Football Association (in German)
CONTENT.Career statistics: As of match played 28 May 2024As of match played 18 November 2020
CONTENT.International career: Ranftl made his debut for Austria national team on 19 November 2019 in a Euro 2020 qualifier against Latvia. He substituted Stefan Ilsanker in the 77th minute.

SUMMARY: Reinhold Ranftl (born 24 January 1992) is an Austrian professional footballer who plays as a right midfielder for Austrian Bundesliga club Austria Wien. He also played for the Austria national team.
